claiming_party_legal_position: Apex Widgets Co.'s claim is invalid.
reasoning_steps: Digital Dreams Inc. made an offer on 2023-01-01 (Rule R1 satisfied). The offer expired on 2023-01-05 (Rule R2).
Apex Widgets Co. responded on 2023-01-03 with 'We accept your offer for 100 widgets at $150 each, but delivery will be by 2023-02-20.'. This modifies the original delivery date of 2023-02-15.

Under Rule R4, any modification constitutes a counter-offer and terminates the original offer. Therefore, Apex Widgets Co.'s response was a counter-offer, and Digital Dreams Inc.'s original offer was terminated.
Digital Dreams Inc. did not provide explicit written confirmation of acceptance for Apex Widgets Co.'s counter-offer. Under Rule R3, silence or performance alone does not constitute acceptance.
Apex Widgets Co. starting manufacturing on 2023-01-10 is an act of performance, but it does not constitute acceptance by Digital Dreams Inc. (Rule R3).
On 2023-01-12, Digital Dreams Inc. communicated they were 'no longer interested'. At this point, no valid contract had been formed between the parties.
Since no contract was formed, there can be no breach by Digital Dreams Inc. (Rule R7).
Therefore, Apex Widgets Co.'s claim for damages is invalid.
